2604.27914.title: Geometry-Calibrated Conformal Abstention for Language Models
2604.27914.paper_summary: When language models lack relevant knowledge for a given query, they frequently generate plausible responses that can be hallucinations, rather than admitting being agnostic about the answer. Retraining models to reward admitting ignorance can lead to overly conservative behaviors and poor generalization due to scarce evaluation benchmarks. We propose a post hoc framework, Conformal Abstention (CA), adapted from conformal prediction (CP) to determine whether to abstain from answering a query. CA provides finite-sample guarantees on both the probability of participation (i.e., not abstaining) and the probability that the generated response is correct. Importantly, the abstention decision relies on prediction confidence rather than the non-conformity scores used in CP, which are intractable for open-ended generation. To better align prediction confidence with the model's ignorance, we introduce a calibration strategy using representation geometry within the model to measure knowledge involvement in shaping the response. Experiments demonstrate that we improve selective answering significantly with 75 percent conditional correctness.
2604.27914.paper_summary_zh: 當語言模型缺乏針對特定查詢的相關知識時，它們經常生成看似合理的回應，這些回應可能是幻覺，而不是承認對答案的無知。重新訓練模型以獎勵承認無知可能導致過於保守的行為和由於評估基準稀缺而造成的糟糕泛化。我們提出了一個事後框架，稱為符合性放棄（Conformal Abstention, CA），該框架改編自符合性預測（Conformal Prediction, CP），用於決定是否放棄回答查詢。CA 提供了有限樣本保證，涵蓋參與的概率（即不放棄）和生成的回應正確的概率。重要的是，放棄決策依賴於預測信心，而不是 CP 中使用的非符合性分數，後者對於開放式生成來說是不可處理的。為了更好地將預測信心與模型的無知對齊，我們引入了一種校準策略，利用模型內的表示幾何來測量知識在塑造回應中的參與程度。實驗表明，我們在有條件的正確性達到 75% 的情況下顯著改善了選擇性回答。
2604.27914.author: Rui Xu et.al.
2604.27914.authors: Rui Xu, Yi Chen, Sihong Xie, Hui Xiong
